bullets.text: Full-year 2022 companywide CR was 95.8, within the 96 target; Personal Lines CR was 96.0, Commercial Lines 91.1, and Property 110.5 including 25.6 points of catastrophe losses; companywide underwriting profit margin was 4.2%, exceeding the 4% target.

Companywide NPW grew 10% to an implied total with Personal Lines up 9%, Commercial Lines up 17% to $9.4 billion, and Property up 8% to $2.4 billion, despite deliberate growth restrictions in the first half of the year.

Full-year loss severity trends were the most severe in at least 40 years driven by inflation, used vehicle prices, and supply chain disruptions; Hurricane Ian alone contributed an estimated $560 million of Personal Lines losses.

Personal auto rates rose about 8% in 2021, an additional ~9% in the first half of 2022, then moderated to ~4% in the second half as profitability improved and competitors began catching up with comparable increases.

Companywide PIF grew 3% for the full year; Personal Lines ended 2022 with growth in both premiums and policies after a mid-year dip, as improved competitiveness relative to rate-hiking peers began attracting new business.

Investment portfolio returned -7.8% for 2022 with fixed-income at -6.6% and equities at -19.4%; no variable dividend was paid in 2022 as the board opted to preserve capital for growth, though the $0.10 quarterly dividend was maintained.

Hurricane Ian was the largest catastrophe in Progressive's history; per-event reinsurance capped retained Property losses at $200 million or 8.8 points; catastrophe losses across all events totaled nearly 26 points of the full-year Property CR.

Companywide expense ratio declined 1.1 points to 18.5 for 2022, aided by media budget reductions; Personal Lines and Property non-acquisition expense ratios each improved by approximately 0.2-0.3 points versus 2021.

Progressive updated its Purpose statement to 'We exist to help people move forward and live fully' and revised its Vision to include 'business owners' and 'other financial needs,' reflecting the broadening destination-company strategy.

The Superstore campaign approached its 200th ad and 15th anniversary; the Replay campaign debuted for football audiences earning a Best-in-Culture designation among Black/African American audiences; advertising spend was cut in H2 to protect the CR target.
